task: I’m putting together a simple plan for a day when I’ll be working from home and may need to switch between a few tasks as the day goes on. What are a few practical things to set up ahead of time so I can keep the work on track if something unexpected comes up?

turns.agent_response:
A few practical things to set up ahead of time:

- **Make a short priority list** for the day: what must get done, what can wait, and what can be dropped if time gets tight.
- **Prepare offline materials**: save the files you’ll need locally, and keep any key references or notes available without internet.
- **Check your backup connection**: if you have a hotspot or alternate internet option, test it before you need it.
- **Turn on autosave/sync**: make sure your work is saving automatically, and confirm the files are actually syncing before you disconnect or move around.
- **Keep a “next step” note**: jot down where you are in each task so you can restart quickly after an interruption.
- **Have a fallback way to send work**: if your usual email or upload method fails, know the backup option you’ll use.
- **Set up a clean workspace**: charger plugged in, essential tabs open, and only the tools you need visible.

If you want, I can turn this into a very short **morning checklist** you can follow in order.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s solid. Pretty much the right setup. I’d already do the backup connection and the “next step” note — those save time when something breaks in the middle of the day. The short priority list matters too. Keeps you from chasing the wrong thing when the schedule gets messy.
